title: What I think about the Exempliary way to live - Hard Core style - Live frugaly

transcript.text: Hello, this video is what I think it's the exemplary way to live.
This is the way everybody should live.
Okay, so imagining I was a normal person with a regular 9-to-5 job and I owned a house.
I was a homeowner and say even I had a family and all this business.
This would be the most exemplary way to live.
I would immediately stop watering my lawn and I would replace it with a rock garden and cactuses
That's completely maintenance free and doesn't need any water.
So that I will not use any water at all, extra water.
The only water I use is for my one shower a day and the water I drink and that's all the water I use.
No water from my land because we're having water problems.
And I would also, now I'm saying exemplary as in everybody should do this.
This is like how everybody should live.
I also wouldn't spend any money on restaurants.
I wouldn't go out.
I think all the restaurants should close down.
Because it takes gas to get to the restaurant and then it takes all these people have to run around and do their stuff.
And I know what you're thinking, oh, but people will be out of work.
Well, yeah, but you replace those jobs with other industries.
I also wouldn't go out to the movies.
Movies are too much.
Stop going to the movies.
Close down all the movie theaters.
And I also, I wouldn't spend any money on alcohol.
And I, I wouldn't go to any sporting events.

And cover all of our houses immediately.
Big, new deal type of a thing.
Like, oh, FDA, FDR.

So that everybody's houses are totally energy, independent, efficient, independent.
And I wouldn't, I'd have a car, but I'd, I'd never drive it for getting around town.
I'd ride my bike around town all the time.
Um, and I wouldn't buy any processed food at all.
So let's put all the candy businesses out of business.
Candy companies out of business.
And Cheetos and Fritos and Ho-Hos and Twinkies and all their stuff.
Completely out of business, don't buy any of that stuff.

No more animal killing.

For that into redeveloping the infrastructure of farming so that we don't farm on the land
At all.
Build buildings for farms and use aeroponics because that uses even less water.

The extra money on?

To get around.

All those, all those industries.
